target.name: Agility Robotics
target.founder: Jonathan Hurst, Damion Shelton, Mikhail Jones

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $5.2B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Agility Robotics, a Series D robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $400M+, is valued here against a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable. The private peer set includes Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The peer-median approach anchors to the median valuation of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, yielding $1.8B. This reflects the central tendency within the $1.0B–$2.3B range observed across the peer set. The stage-progression method applies a typical Series D-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.5x to a $5.2B base derived from total capital raised, implying $7.8B. This method reflects potential value creation expected through the next financing cycle.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $1.5B (low) to $9.0B (high), with a median of $4.8B. The wide dispersion reflects tension between recent-stage peer valuations, which cluster in the $1.8B–$2.0B range for Series D companies, and the stage-progression method, which embeds forward-looking growth assumptions. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiplier for this specific sector and the degree to which Agility's competitive position and product maturity align with or diverge from the peer set.
